title: The significant role of mother tongue language in education

passages.title: The significant role of mother tongue language in education
passages.source_html:
The significant role of mother tongue language in education


A


One consequence of population mobility is an increasing diversity within schools. To illustrate, in the city of Toronto in Canada, 58% of kindergarten pupils come from homes where English is not the language of communication. Schools in Europe and North America have experienced this diversity for years, but educational policies and practices vary widely between countries and even within countries. Some political parties and groups search for ways to ‘solve the problem’ of diverse communities and their integration in schools and society. They see few positive consequences for the host society and worry that diversity threatens the identity of the host society. Consequently, they promote unfortunate educational policies that will make the ‘problem’ disappear. If students retain their culture and language, they are viewed as less capable of identifying with the mainstream culture and learning the mainstream language of the society.


B


The challenge for educators and policy-makers is to shape the evolution of national identity in such a way that the rights of all citizens (including school children) are respected, and the cultural, linguistic and economic resources of the nation are maximized. To waste the resources of the nation by discouraging children from developing their mother tongues is quite simply unintelligent from the point of view of national self-interest. A first step in providing an appropriate education for culturally and linguistically diverse children is to examine what the existing research says about the role of children's mother tongues in their educational development.


C


In fact, the research is very clear. When children continue to develop their abilities in two or more languages throughout primary school, they gain a deeper understanding of language and how to use it effectively. They have more practice in processing language, especially when they develop literacy in both. More than 150 research studies conducted during the past 35 years strongly support what Goethe, the famous eighteenth-century German philosopher, once said: that the person who knows only one language does not truly know that language. Research suggests that bilingual children may also develop more flexibility in their thinking as a result of processing information through two different languages.


D


Children who come to school with a solid foundation in their mother tongue develop stronger literacy abilities in the school language. When parents and other caregivers (e.g. grandparents) are able to spend time with their children and tell stories or discuss issues with them in a way that develops their mother tongue, children come to school well-prepared to learn the school language and succeed educationally. Children's knowledge and skills transfer across languages from the mother tongue to the school language. Transfer across languages can be two-way: both languages nurture each other when the educational environment permits children access to both languages.


E


Some educators and parents are suspicious of mother tongue-based teaching programs because they worry that they take time away from the majority language. For example, in a bilingual program where 50% of the time is spent teaching through children's home language and 50% through the majority language, surely children won't progress as far in the latter? One of the most strongly established findings of educational research, however, is that well-implemented bilingual programs can promote literacy and subject-matter knowledge in a minority language without any negative effects on children's development in the majority language. Within Europe, the Foyer program in Belgium, which develops children's speaking and literacy abilities in three languages (their mother tongue, Dutch and French), most clearly illustrates the benefits of bilingual and trilingual education (see Cummins, 2000).


F


It is easy to understand how this happens. When children are learning through a minority language, they are learning concepts and intellectual skills too. Pupils who know how to tell the time in their mother tongue understand the concept of telling time. In order to tell time in the majority language they do not need to re-learn the concept. Similarly, at more advanced stages, there is transfer across languages in other skills, such as knowing how to distinguish the main idea from the supporting details of a written passage or story, and distinguishing fact from opinion. Studies of secondary school pupils are providing interesting findings in this area, and it would be worth extending this research.


G


Many people marvel at how quickly bilingual children seem to ‘pick up’ conversational skills in the majority language at school (although it takes much longer for them to catch up with native speakers in academic language skills). However, educators are often much less aware of how quickly children can lose their ability to use their mother tongue, even in the home context. The extent and rapidity of language loss will vary according to the concentration of families from a particular linguistic group in the neighborhood. Where the mother tongue is used extensively in the community, then language loss among young children will be less. However, where language communities are not concentrated in particular neighborhoods, children can lose their ability to communicate in their mother tongue within two to three years of starting school. They may retain receptive skills in the language but they will use the majority language in speaking with their peers and siblings and in responding to their parents. By the time children become adolescents, the linguistic division between parents and children has become an emotional chasm. Pupils frequently become alienated from the cultures of both home and school, with predictable results.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27 - 30


Choose the correct answer.

27

What point is the writer making in the second paragraph?

正确答案： C. Bilingual children can make a valuable contribution to the wealth of a country.

定位词：the second paragraph


定位原文： B段，The challenge for educators and policy-makers is to shape the evolution of national identity in such a way that the rights of all citizens (including school children) are respected, and the cultural, linguistic and economic resources of the nation are maximized. To waste the resources of the nation by discouraging children from developing their mother tongues is quite simply unintelligent from the point of view of national self-interest. A first step in providing an appropriate education for culturally and linguistically diverse children is to examine what the existing research says about the role of children's mother tongues in their educational development.


详细解析：

B段指出了讨论如何通过尊重和利用所有公民（包括学生）的文化和语言资源，来最大化国家的经济、文化和语言资源。

28

Why does the writer refer to something that Goethe said? 

正确答案： A. to lend weight to his argument

定位词： Goethe said


定位原文： C段，More than 150 research studies conducted during the past 35 years strongly support what Goethe, the famous eighteenth-century German philosopher, once said: that the person who knows only one language does not truly know that language.


详细解析：

C段指出，Goethe的话是为了支持作者的论点，通过引用哲学家的观点来增强论证的说服力。引用Goethe的名言目的是为了证明学习多语言的重要性，进而为双语教育的价值提供支持。文章并未反驳任何现有的研究，而是通过引用Goethe的话来增强现有研究的有效性。文中并没有引入新的概念，而是引用了一位历史人物的名言来支持观点。文中没有更新现有的理论，引用Goethe的话是为了增加自己论点的权威性。

29

The writer believes that when young children have a firm grasp of their mother tongue

正确答案： B. they go on to do much better throughout their time at school.

定位词： writer believes that, younger children, mother tongue


定位原文： D段，Children who come to school with a solid foundation in their mother tongue develop stronger literacy abilities in the school language.


详细解析：

D段指出，拥有坚实母语基础的孩子能够在学校语言的学习中表现得更好。文中明确表明，母语的掌握对学校学习具有积极的推动作用，因此可以得知，掌握母语的孩子会在学校中表现得更好。文中并没有提到孩子可以教授家庭成员所学的内容，重点是孩子在学校的学习表现。文章并没有提到孩子会读与自己文化背景相关的故事，而是侧重于母语对学校语言学习的帮助。文中并没有讨论孩子和家庭的关系，讨论的主要是孩子在学校的学习进展。


同义替换：firm grasp of = solid foundation in

30

Why are some people suspicious about mother tongue-based teaching programmes?

正确答案： D. They fear that the programmes will use up valuable time in the school day.

定位词： suspicious, mother tongue-based teaching


定位原文： E段，Some educators and parents are suspicious of mother tongue-based teaching programs because they worry that they take time away from the majority language.


详细解析：E段指出，部分教育工作者和家长担心母语教育会占用学习主流语言的时间，这样会影响孩子在主流语言学习中的进度，这种担忧主要来源于对时间分配的忧虑。文中并未提到孩子学习阅读的速度或其他语言技能的进展，讨论的重点在时间管理上。文章没有提到孩子可能会混淆两种语言，而是讨论了时间分配的问题。文中并未讨论孩子对课程的兴趣，而是关注时间如何影响孩子学习主流语言的进度。


同义替换：suspicious = Worry； use up valuable time = Take time away

Questions 31 - 35


Complete the summary using the list of words. Choose the correct word and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. teachers
B. school
C. dislocation
D. rate
E. time
F. family
G. communication
H. type
I. ability
J. area
31

Bilingual children

It has often been noted that bilingual children acquire the to converse in the majority language remarkably quickly.

正确答案： I

定位词： bilingual children, converse, majority language


定位原文： G段，Many people marvel at how quickly bilingual children seem to ‘pick up’ conversational skills in the majority language at school.


详细解析：G段指出，许多人惊叹于双语儿童在学校学习主流语言时，似乎能多快地掌握日常对话技巧。


同义替换： conversational skills = the ability to converse

32

The fact that the mother tongue can disappear at a similar  is less well understood.

正确答案： D

定位词： mother tongue, disappear, similar


定位原文： G段，Many people marvel at how quickly bilingual children seem to ‘pick up’ conversational skills in the majority language at school (although it takes much longer for them to catch up with native speakers in academic language skills). However, educators are often much less aware of how quickly children can lose their ability to use their mother tongue, even in the home context.


详细解析：G段指出许多人惊叹于双语儿童在学校里似乎能如此迅速地“掌握”主流语言的会话技能（尽管他们在学术语言技能方面赶上母语者需要更长时间）。然而，教育者往往不太意识到，孩子们在家庭环境中也能很快失去使用母语的能力。这表明孩子学习主流语言和失去使用母语的能力的速度都非常快。

33

This phenomenon depends to a certain extent on the proportion of people with the same linguistic background that have settled in a particular  .

正确答案： J

定位词： the same linguistic background


定位原文： G段，The extent and rapidity of language loss will vary according to the concentration of families from a particular linguistic group in the neighborhood.


详细解析：G段指出语言丧失的程度和速度会根据特定语言群体在某个社区的家庭集中程度而有所不同。


同义替换：proportion of people = concentration of families

34

If this is limited, children are likely to lose the active use of their mother tongue, and thus no longer employ it even with  although they may still understand it.

正确答案： F

定位词： lose, active use, mother tongue


定位原文： G段，However, where language communities are not concentrated in particular neighborhoods, children can lose their ability to communicate in their mother tongue within two to three years of starting school.


详细解析：G段指出如果某个语言群体的家庭在特定社区中并不集中，那么孩子们可能会在开始上学后的两到三年内，失去使用母语的能力。

35

It follows that teenage children in these circumstances experience a sense of  in relation to all aspects of their lives.

正确答案： C

定位词： teenage children, sense


定位原文： G段，Pupils frequently become alienated from the cultures of both home and school, with predictable results.


详细解析：G段指出，学生常常与家庭和学校的文化产生疏离感，dislocation表示文化上的割裂或疏远。

Questions 36 - 40


Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or choose NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

36

Less than half the children who attend kindergarten in Toronto have English as their mother tongue.

正确答案： A. YES

定位词： kindergarten in Toronto


定位原文： A段，In the city of Toronto in Canada, 58% of kindergarten pupils come from homes where English is not the language of communication.


详细解析：A段指出，58%的幼儿园学生来自非英语家庭，意味着有超过一半的孩子的母语并非英语。

37

Research proves that learning the host country language at school can have an adverse effect on a child's mother tongue.

正确答案： B. NO

定位词： research,  host country language


定位原文： E段，One of the most strongly established findings of educational research, however, is that well-implemented bilingual programs can promote literacy and subject-matter knowledge in a minority language without any negative effects on children's development in the majority language.


详细解析：E段指出，研究表明良好设计实施的双语教育项目可以提升非主流语言的能力，也就是题目中孩子母语的额能力。

38

The Foyer programme is to be adopted by the French education system.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位词： Foyer program, Belgium, Dutch and French


定位原文： E段，Within Europe, the Foyer program in Belgium, which develops children's speaking and literacy abilities in three languages (their mother tongue, Dutch and French), most clearly illustrates the benefits of bilingual and trilingual education (see Cummins, 2000).


详细解析：E段指出Foyer项目旨在提升学生在母语，荷兰语和法语三种语言上的能力。该项目已经在比利时实施并展示了双语和三语教育的优势。而题目中的French education system（法国教育系统）并未提及。

39

Bilingual children are taught to tell the time earlier than monolingual children.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位词： tell the time, bilingual children, monolingual children


定位原文： F段，Pupils who know how to tell the time in their mother tongue understand the concept of telling time.


详细解析：F段指出，掌握时间的概念对于双语儿童来说是一个普遍技能，但文中并未比较双语儿童和单语儿童在学习这一技能的时间差，因此无法判断是否更早学会。

40

Bilingual children can eventually apply reading comprehension strategies acquired in one language when reading in the other.

正确答案： A. YES

定位词：Bilingual children, reading comprehension strategies


定位原文： F段，Similarly, at more advanced stages, there is transfer across languages in other skills, such as knowing how to distinguish the main idea from the supporting details of a written passage or story, and distinguishing fact from opinion.


详细解析：F段指出，双语儿童可以将他们在母语学习中学到的理解策略应用到另一种语言的学习中，包括区分论点和论据以及事实和观点的能力。
